column1: description

The gnutls_session_get_data function in libgnutls 2.12.23 and 3.x before 3.3.8, 3.4.x before 3.4.4, and 3.5.x before 3.5.1, when called with a NULL session_data argument, allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory or cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a crafted session.

The kernel in Apple iOS before 8.1.3 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in a privileged context or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted app, a different vulnerability than CVE-2015-1579.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 and R2 SP1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the handling of a window message, aka "Win32k User Input Handling Vulnerability."

The asn1_write_ContextSimple function in lib/asn1/asn1.c in Samba 4.x before 4.2.10, 4.3.x before 4.3.9, and 4.4.x before 4.4.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read and application crash) via a crafted ASN.1 BER encoding.
The kernel in Android 4.x before 4.4.4 does not properly restrict the use of the SCTP protocol, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

The csum_len_for_type function in net/core/checksum.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 does not validate certain data structures, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory by reading a certain location.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 Gold, SP1, and SP2, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8 Gold and RT allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect pointer value, aka "Windows Kernel Memory Disclosure Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (2) NtUserSetWindowStationUserEx, (3) NtUserSetWindowStation, (4) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (5) NtUserSetWindowStationUserEx, (6) NtUserSetWindowStation, (7) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (8) NtUserSetWindowStationUserEx, (9) NtUserSetWindowStation, (10) NtUserSetWindowStatio

The LDAP server in OpenLDAP 2.4.40 and earlier allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (daemon crash) via a crafted LDAP request, related to the ldap_debug_print_tree function in slapd/slap.c.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (2) NtUserSetWindowStationUserEx, (3) NtUserSwitchDesktop, or (4) NtUserOpenWindowStation API function, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStation and (2) NtUserSetThreadDesktop API functions, aka "Windows User Object Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 Gold and R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a named pipe, as exploited in the wild in November 2014, aka "Win32k Named Pipe Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 Gold and R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect check for the presence of a null pointer, aka "Windows Kernel Null Pointer Dereference Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute and (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function calls, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The mod_exp implementation in OpenSSL before 1.0.1m and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2h, when the modulus is zero, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a crafted certificate.

The Linux kernel through 4.14.11 mishandles the handling of the PTRACE_TRACEME request by a process, which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a PTRACE_ATTACH operation by the parent process, related to ptrace.c and ptrace.h.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 does not properly initialize a certain structure member, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel memory or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted application, aka "Windows Kernel Memory Disclosure Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) CreateProcessWithLogonW or (2) CreateProcessAsUser API function, aka "Windows Create Process Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Apple iOS before 8.1.3 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in a privileged context or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted app, a different vulnerability than CVE-2015-1579.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute and (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function calls, aka "Windows User Objects Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The zend_ts_hash_graceful_destroy function in Zend/zend_hash.c in PHP before 5.4.39, 5.5.x before 5.5.21, and 5.6.x before 5.6.3 does not properly initialize a certain mutex, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a named pipe, as exploited in the wild in November 2014, aka "Win32k Named Pipe Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."
The XRRGetOutputInfo function in X11R7 before 1.13.3 and 1.14.x before 1.14.3 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and crash) via a crafted X11 request.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the creation of a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The Linux kernel before 4.10.11 does not properly initialize a certain structure member, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory by reading an uninitialized value.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 and R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the creation of a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Creation Race Condition Vulnerability."

The TLS implementation in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1s and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2h does not properly consider timing side-channels during the processing of (1) a CBC padding error, (2) a version rollback attack, or (3) a ciphersuite downgrade attack, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information via statistical analysis of timing data in a crafted TLS handshake, aka the "Heartbleed" bug.

The Xv extension in the XFree86 X server before 4.4.99.1 does not properly initialize a certain structure member, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and server crash) via a crafted XvPutImage request.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a named pipe, as exploited in the wild in November 2015, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect check for the presence of a file, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The g_signal_emit function in gobject/gobject.c in the GLib library, as used in the GStreamer 0.10.36 and 1.4.5, and other products, does not properly initialize a certain data structure, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted signal.

The g_quark_from_static_string function in GLib before 2.44.1 does not properly handle a NULL argument, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, related to glib/gquark.c.

The my_object_increment_retval_error function in GObject-2.0 before 2.44.1 does not properly validate error arguments, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted application, related to gobject.c and gparam.c.

The my_object_increment_val function in the MyObject class in the GObject library in GLib before 2.40.2 does not properly validate the obj argument, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a NULL pointer.

The g_value_set_int function in gobject/gvalue.c in GLib before 2.44.1 does not properly validate the type of a GValue, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted serialized GValue object, related to the G_TYPE_INT type.

The g_value_unset function in gobject/gvalue.c in the GLib library, as used in the GStreamer gst-plugins-base package before 1.2.4, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted gst_value_set_string_take function call, which triggers a use-after-free.

The kernel in Apple iOS before 8.1.3 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in a privileged context or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted app, a different vulnerability than CVE-2015-1538.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1 and R2 SP1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the handling of a (1) CreateProcessWithLogonW or (2) CreateProcessAsUserW API call, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The SSLv23_method() function in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1p and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2h, when SSL_OP_NO_SSLv2 is not set, does not properly maintain the SSLv2 handshake state, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a series of crafted handshakes, as demonstrated by a client that sends a series of SSLv2 CLIENT-HELLO messages.

The SSL/TLS implementation in OpenSSL 1.0.1 before 1.0.1s and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2d does not properly handle a crafted DTLS handshake message, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (buffer overflow and application crash) via a large number of crafted records, aka the "DTLS handshake flood" issue.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the creation of a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute and (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function calls, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 Gold and R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect check for a NULL pointer, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute and (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function calls, aka "Windows User Objects Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The SSL/TLS protocol 3.0 and earlier, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1m and other products, does not properly handle a series of handshake messages, which allows man-in-the-middle attackers to cause a denial of service (infinite loop) via a series of crafted handshake messages, aka the "CCS Injection" vulnerability.

The SSL/TLS protocol 3.0 and earlier, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1m and other products, does not properly consider whether a certain "plaintext" value is potentially malleable when this value is used to compute an HMAC signature, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (server crash) via a crafted handshake, related to s3_srvr.c and t1_lib.c, aka the "CCS Injection" issue.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 does not properly validate the length of a user-mode buffer, which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka "Windows Kernel-Mode Drivers Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) CreateProcessWithLogonW or (2) CreateProcessAsUser API function, aka "Windows Create Process Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStation and (2) NtUserSetThreadDesktop functions, aka "Windows User Object Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, as exploited in the wild in November 2014, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The gs_lib_ctx_init function in base/gslibctx.c in Artifex Software, Inc. Ghostscript before 9.18 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a crafted PostScript document.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) CreateProcessWithLogonW or (2) CreateProcessAsUser API, aka "Windows Create Process Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages use of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (2) NtUserSwitchDesktop, or (3) NtUserOpenWindowStation API function, as exploited in the wild in November 2014, aka "Win32k User Mode Write AV Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) CreateProcessWithLogonW or (2) CreateProcessAsUser API function, aka "Windows Create Process Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, Windows 8.1, and Windows Server 2012 R2 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages the ability to create a process with a token that has a modified integrity level, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The qcow2_snapshot_load_tmp function in drivers/block/qcow2.c in QEMU 2.0.0 and 1.5.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and application crash) via a crafted qcow2 image.

The Linux kernel before 4.10.11 does not properly initialize the ctl_table.proc_handler field of the ctl_table structure, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel memory or cause a denial of service (system crash) by reading the /proc/sys/net/ipv4/tcp_allowed_congestion_control file.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages an incorrect check for the presence of a certain token in a process's token list, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8.1 Gold and 8.1 Update through 1 does not properly handle a crafted request to the Windows kernel-mode driver (win32k.sys), which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages access to a shared library, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The cipso_v4_req_delattr function in net/ipv4/cipso_ipv4.c in the Linux kernel before 3.19.3 does not initialize a certain structure member, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory by leveraging the CAP_NET_RAW capability for a SO_GET_TXTIME socket option.

The Linux kernel through 4.14.10 mishandles the interaction between the use of the mmap system call with the MAP_GROWSDOWN flag and the use of the mprotect system call, which allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging a lack of certain mprotect permission checks, as demonstrated by the bcc tool, aka a "double-fetch" issue.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 Gold, SP1, and SP2, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8 Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the handling of a certain IRP, aka "Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE registry key, aka "Windows Registry Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages use of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute or (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The xfrm6_tunnel_spi_lookup function in net/ipv6/xfrm6_tunnel.c in the Linux kernel before 4.10 does not properly initialize a certain structure member, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory by reading an uninitialized value.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowCompositionAttribute and (2) NtUserSetWindowCompositionTransition function return values, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The Linux kernel through 4.10.1 mishandles the interaction between the mmap system call and the MAP_FIXED flag, which allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging the ability to make a process call mmap with a certain address, as demonstrated by the CVE-2017-7308 exploit for the Linux kernel, aka a "double-fault vulnerability."

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

The __strcpy_chk function in the GNU C Library (aka glibc or libc6) through 2.23 does not provide a NULL pointer check, which allows context-dependent attackers to obtain sensitive process information from uninitialized stack data via a crafted application, related to wcsmbs/wcschr.c and string/str-two-way.h.

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

The kernel in Apple OS X before 10.11.1 allows local users to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted app that leverages a race condition involving a (1) mach_port_allocate or (2) mach_port_construct kernel call and a (3) mach_port_insert_right or (4) mach_port_construct_insert_right kernel call.

The nfs_set_open_stateid function in fs/nfs/nfs4state.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact by leveraging incorrect handling of a certain stateid.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2003 SP2, Windows Vista SP2, Windows Server 2008 SP2 and R2 SP1, Windows 7 SP1, Windows 8, Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows RT Gold and 8.1 allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages incorrect handling of the (1) NtUserSetWindowStationUser, (2) NtUserSetThreadState, and (3) NtUserOpenWindowStation API functions, aka "Win32k Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability."

The nfs4_close_sync function in fs/nfs/nfs4file.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) via a crafted NFSv4 close operation.

The bnep_net_setup function in net/bluetooth/bnep_net.c in the Linux kernel before 3.19.2 does not properly initialize the netdev_ops structure, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory or cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted application.

The kernel in Apple iOS before 9.3.2 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in a privileged context or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted app, a different vulnerability than CVE-2016-1539, CVE-2016-1540, and CVE-2016-1541.

The kernel in Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows 7 Gold, SP1, and SP2, Windows Server 2012 Gold and R2, and Windows 8 Gold and RT allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application that leverages a race condition during the creation of a window station, aka "Win32k Window Station Creation Race Condition Vulnerability."
